items.title: Hollinger POTD Matt Harpring. Georgia Tech SF was drafted 15th in 98 by ORL. Role player as rook but missed most of 2nd yr w/injury. After unhappy 01-02 w/PHI turned into key cog for UTA, averaging 16 ppg in 3 yrs as starter. Good bench guy from 06-09. Has done TV work since.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst

items.title: had some time this weekend to *manually* write some code to make a chart and it was great!
items.author: shri
items.body_excerpt:
had some time this weekend to *manually* write some code to make a chart and it was great! 

this is a lil self-tracked data viz about my month walking

it's a bit over the top, but for my audience of one, I really like the quintuple-encoded dots (temperature, steps, humidity, rain, and date)

images.alt: A scatterplot of shri's step goals in May, temperature on the y-axis and step count on the x-axis

items.author: Sports Media Watch
items.body_excerpt:
NBC primetime this weekend last year: 
• Saturday: Reruns of "Destination X" and "Yes Chef," plus "Dateline Mystery"
• Sunday: Reruns of "American Ninja Warrior" and "America's Got Talent"

This year:
• Saturday: NBA WCF Game 7
• Sunday: Cubs-Cardinals Sunday Night Baseball

items.title: My latest for @orlandosentinel.com: Headed back to the Finals, the Spurs are “thrilled” for incoming #Magic coach Sean Sweeney:
items.author: Jason Beede
items.body_excerpt:
My latest for @orlandosentinel.com: Headed back to the Finals, the Spurs are “thrilled” for incoming #Magic coach Sean Sweeney:

“Happy for Orlando,” San Antonio coach Mitch Johnson said Saturday. “It’s going to be a really good fit and well-deserved…”

More: www.orlandosentinel.com/2026/05/31/o...

media.title: Incoming Magic coach Sean Sweeney headed to NBA Finals with Spurs
media.description: This is Sean Sweeney’s second time reaching the NBA Finals as an assistant coach, something the incoming Orlando Magic coach first did with the Dallas Mavericks in 2024.

items.title: Highest True Shooting Percentage among all players who averaged more than 19 PPG over the first 12 games of a postseason:
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Highest True Shooting Percentage among all players who averaged more than 19 PPG over the first 12 games of a postseason:

1. OG Anunoby: 72.4%
2. Kevin McHale: 70.5%
3. Manu Ginobili: 68.5% 
4. Steph Curry: 67.5%
5. James Worthy: 67.4%
6. Kevin Durant 67.3%
7. Kawhi Leonard: 67.2%
